mnemonics: IAD (Information, Analysis, Design): Think of the project phases as a triad that must be balanced.
UC (Use Cases): Remember 'Users Communicate' to recall that use cases capture user-system interactions.
keyPoints: The primary goal of analysis is to understand the 'what' (user requirements), not the 'how' (implementation).
Information classes represent the key entities or 'nouns' of the system domain (e.g., Customer, Order, Product).
Analysis classes (like Boundary, Controller, Entity) help to create a robust object-oriented model that is resilient to changes in UI or business logic.
commonMistakes: Confusing information classes (domain concepts) with database tables (implementation details).
Defining 'how' the system will work during analysis, instead of focusing on 'what' the system must do.
